        Code Changes (39)
        
1. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java
Before
/**
 * A record batch is a container for records. In old versions of the record format (versions 0 and 1),
 * a batch consisted always of a single record if no compression was enabled, but could contain
 * many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records
 * regardless of compression.
 */
public interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {
After
/**
 * A record batch is a container for one or more {@link Record} objects and is the fundamental unit of
 * data transfer in the Kafka wire protocol. Producers write batches to partition leaders; brokers
 * replicate, validate, and store them; consumers fetch and deserialize them.
 *
 * <p>The Kafka record format has evolved across three magic versions:
 * <ul>
 *   <li><b>Magic v0</b> ({@link #MAGIC_VALUE_V0}) — original message format; no timestamps, no compression
 *       across multiple messages in a single logical unit.</li>
 *   <li><b>Magic v1</b> ({@link #MAGIC_VALUE_V1}) — added per-record timestamps and outer-record
 *       compression, but a batch with no compression still contained exactly one record.</li>
 *   <li><b>Magic v2</b> ({@link #MAGIC_VALUE_V2}, {@link #CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE}) — redesigned batch
 *       format with a fixed batch header, variable-length delta-encoded records, efficient CRC-32C
 *       checksumming, and first-class support for idempotent and transactional producers.</li>
 * </ul>
 *
 * <p>Implementations carry read-only access to serialized bytes (e.g. a slice of a
 * {@link java.nio.ByteBuffer}). Callers should iterate records via {@link #iterator()} for simple
 * use cases or via {@link #streamingIterator(org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier)} for
 * memory-efficient streaming decompression. The streaming iterator <em>must</em> be closed after use.
 *
 * <p><b>Thread safety:</b> Implementations of this interface are <em>not</em> guaranteed to be
 * thread-safe. {@link DefaultRecordBatch} instances wrap a shared {@link java.nio.ByteBuffer} and
 * must not be accessed concurrently from multiple threads without external synchronization.
 *
 * @see DefaultRecordBatch
 * @see MemoryRecords
 * @see Record
 */
public interfac
... (truncated)
2. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java
Before
    /**
     * Does the batch have a valid producer id set.
     */
    boolean hasProducerId();
After
    /**
     * Check whether this batch has a valid producer id set (i.e. the producer id is not
     * {@link #NO_PRODUCER_ID}).
     *
     * @return {@code true} if a valid producer id is present, {@code false} otherwise
     */
    boolean hasProducerId();
3. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java
Before
    /**
     * Check whether this record batch is compressed.
     * @return true if so, false otherwise
     */
    boolean isCompressed();
After
    /**
     * Check whether this record batch uses compression.
     *
     * <p>When a batch is compressed, individual records are not accessible without decompression.
     * Use {@link #streamingIterator(org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier)} to decompress
     * lazily one record at a time, or {@link #iterator()} to decompress the entire batch eagerly.
     *
     * @return {@code true} if the batch uses a non-{@link CompressionType#NONE} codec, {@code false} otherwise
     */
    boolean isCompressed();
4. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java
Before
    /**
     * Write this record batch into a buffer.
     * @param buffer The buffer to write the batch to
     */
    void writeTo(ByteBuffer buffer);
After
    /**
     * Serialize this record batch into the given buffer.
     *
     * <p>The buffer must have sufficient {@link ByteBuffer#remaining()} capacity to hold
     * {@link #sizeInBytes()} bytes. The batch is written starting at the current position
     * and the position is advanced by the number of bytes written.
     *
     * @param buffer the destination buffer; must have at least {@link #sizeInBytes()} bytes remaining
     */
    void writeTo(ByteBuffer buffer);
5. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java
Before
    /**
     * Whether or not this record batch is part of a transaction.
     * @return true if it is, false otherwise
     */
    boolean isTransactional();
After
    /**
     * Check whether this record batch is part of a transaction.
     *
     * <p>Transactional batches are produced by producers that have opened a transaction using
     * the transactional API. Consumers configured with {@code isolation.level=read_committed}
     * will not expose records from transactional batches until the corresponding transaction is
     * committed (i.e. an {@link EndTransactionMarker} with {@code COMMIT} is received).
     *
     * @return {@code true} if the transactional flag is set in the batch attributes, {@code false} otherwise
     */
    boolean isTransactional();
6. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java
Before
    /**
     * Check whether this is a control batch (i.e. whether the control bit is set in the batch attributes).
     * For magic versions prior to 2, this is always false.
     *
     * @return Whether this is a batch containing control records
     */
    boolean isControlBatch();
After
    /**
     * Check whether this is a control batch (i.e. whether the control bit is set in the batch attributes).
     *
     * <p>Control batches contain internal broker records such as transaction markers
     * ({@link EndTransactionMarker}) and KRaft metadata entries. They are not exposed to
     * application-level consumers. For magic versions prior to 2, this is always {@code false}.
     *
     * @return {@code true} if the control flag is set in the batch attributes, {@code false} otherwise
     */
    boolean isControlBatch();
7. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java
Before
    /**
     * iterate all records to find the offset of max timestamp.
     * noted:
     * 1) that the earliest offset will return if there are multi records having same (max) timestamp
     * 2) it always returns None if the {@link RecordBatch#magic()} is equal to {@link RecordBatch#MAGIC_VALUE_V0}
     * @return offset of max timestamp
     */
    default Optional<Long> offsetOfMaxTimestamp() {
After
    /**
     * Iterate all records in this batch to find the offset of the record with the maximum timestamp.
     *
     * <p>Notes:
     * <ul>
     *   <li>If multiple records share the same maximum timestamp, the <em>earliest</em> offset among
     *       them is returned.</li>
     *   <li>This method always returns {@link Optional#empty()} for magic v0 batches because
     *       {@link #MAGIC_VALUE_V0} does not carry per-record timestamps.</li>
     *   <li>The method decompresses the batch on each call; callers in performance-sensitive paths
     *       should cache the result.</li>
     * </ul>
     *
     * @return an {@link Optional} containing the offset of the record with the maximum timestamp,
     *         or {@link Optional#empty()} if the batch is magic v0 or contains no records with a
     *         timestamp matching {@link #maxTimestamp()}
     */
    default Optional<Long> offsetOfMaxTimestamp() {
8. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/DefaultRecordBatch.java
Before
/**
 * RecordBatch implementation for magic 2 and above. The schema is given below:
 *
 * RecordBatch =>
 *  BaseOffset => Int64
 *  Length => Int32
 *  PartitionLeaderEpoch => Int32
 *  Magic => Int8
 *  CRC => Uint32
 *  Attributes => Int16
 *  LastOffsetDelta => Int32 // also serves as LastSequenceDelta
 *  BaseTimestamp => Int64
 *  MaxTimestamp => Int64
 *  ProducerId => Int64
 *  ProducerEpoch => Int16
 *  BaseSequence => Int32
 *  RecordsCount => Int32
 *  Records => [Record]
 *
 * Note that when compression is enabled (see attributes below), the compressed record data is serialized
 * directly following the count of the number of records.
 *
 * The CRC covers the data from the attributes to the end of the batch (i.e. all the bytes that follow the CRC). It is
 * located after the magic byte, which means that clients must parse the magic byte before deciding how to interpret
 * the bytes between the batch length and the magic byte. The partition leader epoch field is not included in the CRC
 * computation to avoid the need to recompute the CRC when this field is assigned for every batch that is received by
 * the broker. The CRC-32C (Castagnoli) polynomial is used for the computation.
 *
 * On Compaction: Unlike the older message formats, magic v2 and above preserves the first and last offset/sequence
 * numbers from the original batch when the log is cleaned. This is required in order to be able to restore the
 * producer's state when the log is reloaded. If we did not retain the last sequence number, then following
 * a partition leader failure, once the new leader has rebuilt the producer state from the log, the next sequence
 * expected number would no longer be in sync with what was written by the client. This would cause an
 * unexpected OutOfOrderSequence e
... (truncated)
After
/**
 * Concrete {@link RecordBatch} implementation for magic version 2 and above (the current Kafka
 * record format). Instances wrap a read-only slice of a {@link java.nio.ByteBuffer} that holds the
 * complete, serialized representation of a single batch as it appears on the wire or on disk.
 *
 * <h2>Binary layout</h2>
 * <pre>{@code
 * RecordBatch =>
 *   BaseOffset            => Int64
 *   Length                => Int32
 *   PartitionLeaderEpoch  => Int32
 *   Magic                 => Int8
 *   CRC                   => Uint32
 *   Attributes            => Int16
 *   LastOffsetDelta       => Int32   // also serves as LastSequenceDelta
 *   BaseTimestamp         => Int64
 *   MaxTimestamp          => Int64
 *   ProducerId            => Int64
 *   ProducerEpoch         => Int16
 *   BaseSequence          => Int32
 *   RecordsCount          => Int32
 *   Records               => [Record]
 * }</pre>
 *
 * <p>When compression is enabled, the compressed record bytes are serialized directly after
 * the {@code RecordsCount} field. The CRC covers every byte from {@code Attributes} to the
 * end of the batch using the CRC-32C (Castagnoli) polynomial. The {@code PartitionLeaderEpoch}
 * field is intentionally excluded from the CRC to avoid recomputing it on every batch received
 * by the broker.
 *
 * <h2>Batch attributes (bits 0–6 of the {@code Attributes} field)</h2>
 * <pre>{@code
 * | Unused (7-15) | Delete Horizon Flag (6) | Control (5) | Transactional (4) | Timestamp Type (3) | Compression Type (0-2) |
 * }</pre>
 *
 * <h2>Compaction semantics</h2>
 * Unlike the older message formats, magic v2 preserves the first and last offset and sequence
 * numbers from the original batch through log compaction. This is necessary to correctly restore
 * idempotent producer state aft
... (truncated)
9. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/DefaultRecordBatch.java
Before
    @Override
    public void writeTo(ByteBufferOutputStream outputStream) {
        outputStream.write(this.buffer.duplicate());
    }
After
    /**
     * Serialize this record batch into the given {@link ByteBufferOutputStream}.
     *
     * <p>The batch bytes are written starting at the stream's current position. The underlying
     * buffer is duplicated before writing so the internal state of this batch is not modified.
     * This method may cause the output stream to expand its backing buffer if insufficient
     * capacity is available.
     *
     * @param outputStream the destination stream; must not be {@code null}
     */
    @Override
    public void writeTo(ByteBufferOutputStream outputStream) {
        outputStream.write(this.buffer.duplicate());
    }
10. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/DefaultRecordBatch.java
Before
    public InputStream recordInputStream(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier) {
        final ByteBuffer buffer = this.buffer.duplicate();
        buffer.position(RECORDS_OFFSET);
        return Compression.of(compressionType()).build().wrapForInput(buffer, magic(), bufferSupplier);
    }
After
    /**
     * Open a decompressing {@link InputStream} positioned at the first record byte of this batch.
     *
     * <p>For uncompressed batches the returned stream reads directly from the backing buffer.
     * For compressed batches the stream transparently decompresses data using the codec indicated
     * by {@link #compressionType()}. Temporary decompression buffers are allocated through the
     * supplied {@link BufferSupplier}, which should be a pooling supplier in hot paths to avoid
     * per-batch allocations (e.g. LZ4 requires a 64 KB working buffer by default).
     *
     * <p><b>The caller is responsible for closing the returned stream.</b> Failing to do so may
     * leak native or heap memory held by the decompression codec.
     *
     * @param bufferSupplier supplier used to obtain decompression working buffers; must not be {@code null}
     * @return an {@link InputStream} over the (possibly decompressed) record bytes
     */
    public InputStream recordInputStream(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier) {
        final ByteBuffer buffer = this.buffer.duplicate();
        buffer.position(RECORDS_OFFSET);
        return Compression.of(compressionType()).build().wrapForInput(buffer, magic(), bufferSupplier);
    }
11. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/DefaultRecordBatch.java
Before
    @Override
    public CloseableIterator<Record> skipKeyValueIterator(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier) {
After
    /**
     * Return an iterator that skips key, value, and header deserialization for compressed batches,
     * providing access only to the record metadata (offset, timestamp, sequence number).
     *
     * <p>For compressed batches this uses a streaming approach that avoids materializing the full
     * decompressed byte array into a list, making it more memory-efficient than {@link #iterator()}
     * when only metadata is needed (e.g. during log validation). For uncompressed batches the skip
     * optimization is not applied because {@link java.nio.ByteBuffer#position(int)} is less efficient
     * than simply reading the bytes; the regular uncompressed iterator is returned instead.
     *
     * <p><b>The caller must close the returned iterator</b> to release the decompression stream.
     *
     * @param bufferSupplier supplier used to obtain decompression working buffers for compressed batches;
     *                       must not be {@code null}
     * @return a {@link CloseableIterator} over the records in this batch, skipping key/value/headers
     *         for compressed batches
     */
    @Override
    public CloseableIterator<Record> skipKeyValueIterator(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier) {
12. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/DefaultRecordBatch.java
Before
    @Override
    public void setLastOffset(long offset) {
        buffer.putLong(BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET, offset - lastOffsetDelta());
    }
After
    /**
     * Overwrite the base offset stored in the batch header so that the batch's last offset
     * equals the given value.
     *
     * <p>The last offset is expressed as {@code baseOffset + lastOffsetDelta}; therefore
     * this method writes {@code offset - lastOffsetDelta} as the new {@code baseOffset}.
     * The CRC is <em>not</em> recomputed. This method is intended for broker-side offset
     * assignment before a batch is appended to the log.
     *
     * @param offset the desired last offset for this batch; must be &ge; {@code lastOffsetDelta}
     */
    @Override
    public void setLastOffset(long offset) {
        buffer.putLong(BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET, offset - lastOffsetDelta());
    }

    @Override
    public void setMaxTimestamp(TimestampType timestampType, long maxTimestamp) {
After
    /**
     * Update the {@code MaxTimestamp} and {@code Attributes} fields in the batch header, then
     * recompute and store the CRC-32C checksum.
     *
     * <p>If the timestamp type and timestamp value are both unchanged from their current values,
     * this method returns immediately without modifying the buffer or recomputing the CRC.
     * Otherwise, the attributes byte is updated to reflect {@code timestampType}, the
     * {@code MaxTimestamp} field is set to {@code maxTimestamp}, and the CRC is recalculated
     * to cover the modified header and record bytes.
     *
     * <p>This method is used by the broker when assigning {@link org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType#LOG_APPEND_TIME}
     * to a batch on append.
     *
     * @param timestampType the new timestamp type; must not be
     *                      {@link org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType#NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE}
     * @param maxTimestamp  the new maximum timestamp in milliseconds since epoch
     */
    @Override
    public void setMaxTimestamp(TimestampType timestampType, long maxTimestamp) {

    /**
     * Overwrite the {@code PartitionLeaderEpoch} field in the batch header.

     * <p>The partition leader epoch field is excluded from the CRC computation and therefore
     * this method does <em>not</em> recompute the checksum. The broker sets this field when
     * it assigns an epoch to a batch on append.

     *              to indicate an unknown epoch

    @Override
    public void setPartitionLeaderEpoch(int epoch) {

    }

    /**
     * Write a magic v2 batch header with zero records into {@code buffer} at its current position.
     *
     * <p>Empty batch headers are used by the broker to preserve idempotent producer sequence
     * information after all records in a batch have been removed by log compaction. The CRC-32C
     * checksum is computed and embedded in the written header. The buffer's position is advanced
     * to {@code RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD} bytes past its initial position upon return.

Before
    public static int sizeInBytes(long baseOffset, Iterable<Record> records) {
After
    /**
     * Calculate the serialized size in bytes for a magic v2 batch containing the given records,
     * including the {@link #RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD} header.
     *
     * <p>Timestamps within the batch are encoded as deltas relative to the timestamp of the first
     * record; offsets are encoded as deltas relative to {@code baseOffset}. The size returned by
     * this method reflects the uncompressed (worst-case) layout, as compression ratios cannot be
     * predicted without actually compressing the data.
     *
     * @param baseOffset the base offset of the batch; used to compute per-record offset deltas
     * @param records    the records to be serialized; must not be {@code null}
     * @return the total uncompressed size in bytes, or {@code 0} if {@code records} is empty
     */
    public static int sizeInBytes(long baseOffset, Iterable<Record> records) {

    public static int incrementSequence(int sequence, int increment) {
After
    /**
     * Increment an idempotent producer sequence number by {@code increment}, wrapping around
     * {@link Integer#MAX_VALUE} according to Kafka's modular sequence arithmetic.
     *
     * <p>Kafka sequence numbers are stored as 32-bit signed integers but are treated as unsigned
     * values that wrap from {@link Integer#MAX_VALUE} back to {@code 0}. This method implements
     * that wrap-around correctly.
     *
     * @param sequence  the current sequence number; must be non-negative
     * @param increment the amount to add; must be non-negative
     * @return the incremented sequence number, possibly wrapped around {@link Integer#MAX_VALUE}
     */

/**

After
/**
 * An immutable, in-memory {@link Records} implementation backed by a single {@link java.nio.ByteBuffer}
 * containing one or more serialized {@link RecordBatch record batches}.
 *
 * <p>{@code MemoryRecords} is the primary carrier of record data in the Kafka client stack. It is
 * used on the producer side (after {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder} closes a batch), on the broker
 * side (while replicating and compacting data), and on the consumer side (when deserializing fetch
 * responses). The class provides:
 * <ul>
 *   <li>Zero-copy serialization to a {@link java.nio.channels.GatheringByteChannel} via
 *       {@link #writeTo(org.apache.kafka.common.network.TransferableChannel, int, int)}.</li>
 *   <li>Lazy, streaming iteration over {@link MutableRecordBatch} instances through
 *       {@link #batches()} — no decompression occurs until individual records are requested.</li>
 *   <li>In-place record filtering and log compaction support through
 *       {@link #filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier)}.</li>
 *   <li>A family of static factory methods ({@code readableRecords}, {@code withRecords},
 *       {@code withTransactionalRecords}, etc.) covering common creation patterns.</li>
 * </ul>
 *
 * <p>To build a new {@code MemoryRecords} instance from scratch (i.e. when appending records),
 * use {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder} directly or via the
 * {@link #builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long)} family of overloads.
 * To wrap an existing serialized buffer for reading, use {@link #readableRecords(ByteBuffer)}.
 *
 * <h2>Thread safety</h2>
 * {@code MemoryRecords} is <em>not thread-safe</em>. The backing {@link java.nio.ByteBuffer} is
 * shared and its state (limit, position via duplicates) must not be modifi
... (truncated)

    /**
     * Return an iterator over the {@link MutableRecordBatch} instances in this record set.
     *
     * <p>Each call creates a new {@link RecordBatchIterator} backed by a duplicate of the
     * underlying buffer, so multiple independent iterators may be active concurrently as long as
     * no thread mutates the buffer. Iteration is lazy: individual batch headers are parsed on
     * demand and records within a batch are not decompressed until explicitly requested through
     * the batch's own iterator.
     *
     * @return a fresh {@link AbstractIterator} over the batches in this record set; never {@code null}
     */
    @Override
    public AbstractIterator<MutableRecordBatch> batchIterator() {
        return new RecordBatchIterator<>(new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer.duplicate(), Integer.MAX_VALUE));
    }

    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,
                                               Compression compression,

After
    /**
     * Create a {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder} for a new record batch using the current magic version,
     * with no producer identity information and no partition leader epoch.
     *
     * <p>This is the simplest builder factory and is suitable for non-idempotent, non-transactional
     * producers. For transactional or idempotent batches, use one of the overloads that accept a
     * {@code producerId}, {@code producerEpoch}, and {@code baseSequence}.
     *
     * @param buffer          destination buffer; the builder writes the batch header and record bytes
     *                        directly into this buffer starting at its current position
     * @param compression     compression algorithm to apply to record bytes
     * @param timestampType   timestamp semantics ({@link org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType#CREATE_TIME}
     *                        or {@link org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType#LOG_APPEND_TIME})
     * @param baseOffset      first logical offset to assign to records appended to this builder
     * @return a new {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder} ready to accept records
     */
    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,
                                               Compression compression,
                                               TimestampType timestampType,
                                               long baseOffset) {
        return builder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, timestampType, baseOffset);
    }

    /**
     * Build a {@code MemoryRecords} containing all the given {@link SimpleRecord} instances in a
     * single batch using the current magic version, {@link org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType#CREATE_TIME},
     * and base offset {@code 0}.
     *
     * <p>This convenience method allocates a single appropriately-sized buffer, writes the batch,
     * and returns the resulting {@code MemoryRecords}. If {@code records} is empty,
     * {@link #EMPTY} is returned.
     *
     * @param compression compression codec to apply to the record payload
     * @param records     records to include; if empty, {@link #EMPTY} is returned
     * @return a new {@code MemoryRecords} containing the given records
     */
    public static MemoryRecords withRecords(Compression compression, SimpleRecord... records) {
        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, records);
    }

    /**
     * Build a {@code MemoryRecords} containing the given records in a single idempotent batch
     * using the current magic version, base offset {@code 0}, and
     * {@link org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType#CREATE_TIME}.
     *
     * <p>Idempotent batches carry a {@code producerId}, {@code producerEpoch}, and
     * {@code baseSequence} that allow the broker to detect and discard duplicate Produce requests.
     *
     * @param compression   compression codec to apply to the record payload
     * @param producerId    idempotent producer id
     * @param producerEpoch producer epoch
     * @param baseSequence  first sequence number for this batch
     * @param records       records to include; if empty, {@link #EMPTY} is returned
     * @return a new {@code MemoryRecords} containing the given idempotent records
     */
    public static MemoryRecords withIdempotentRecords(Compression compression, long producerId,
                                                      short producerEpoch, int baseSequence, SimpleRecord... records) {
31. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/MemoryRecords.java
Before
    public static MemoryRecords withTransactionalRecords(Compression compression, long producerId,
                                                         short producerEpoch, int baseSequence, SimpleRecord... records) {
After
    /**
     * Build a {@code MemoryRecords} containing the given records in a single transactional batch
     * using the current magic version, base offset {@code 0}, and
     * {@link org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType#CREATE_TIME}.
     *
     * <p>Transactional batches have the transactional flag set in their attributes and are only
     * exposed to consumers configured with {@code isolation.level=read_committed} after the
     * corresponding transaction is committed.
     *
     * @param compression   compression codec to apply to the record payload
     * @param producerId    transactional producer id
     * @param producerEpoch producer epoch
     * @param baseSequence  first sequence number for this batch
     * @param records       records to include; if empty, {@link #EMPTY} is returned
     * @return a new {@code MemoryRecords} containing the given transactional records
     */
    public static MemoryRecords withTransactionalRecords(Compression compression, long producerId,
                                                         short producerEpoch, int baseSequence, SimpleRecord... records) {
32. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/MemoryRecords.java
Before
    public static MemoryRecords withEndTransactionMarker(long producerId, short producerEpoch, EndTransactionMarker marker) {
After
    /**
     * Build a {@code MemoryRecords} containing a single end-of-transaction control batch with a
     * current-time timestamp, base offset {@code 0}, and no partition leader epoch.
     *
     * <p>The resulting batch has the control flag and the transactional flag set. It signals to
     * the broker that the transaction identified by {@code producerId}/{@code producerEpoch} has
     * ended with the result encoded in {@code marker} (commit or abort).
     *
     * @param producerId    the producer id of the transaction being ended
     * @param producerEpoch the producer epoch of the transaction being ended
     * @param marker        the end-of-transaction marker specifying commit or abort
     * @return a new {@code MemoryRecords} containing the end-transaction control batch
     */
    public static MemoryRecords withEndTransactionMarker(long producerId, short producerEpoch, EndTransactionMarker marker) {
33. EDIT /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/MemoryRecords.java
Before
    public static void writeEndTransactionalMarker(ByteBuffer buffer, long initialOffset, long timestamp,
                                                   int partitionLeaderEpoch, long producerId, short producerEpoch,
                                                   EndTransactionMarker marker) {
After
    /**
     * Write a single end-of-transaction control batch directly into {@code buffer} at its current
     * position without allocating a new buffer.
     *
     * <p>The batch has the control flag and the transactional flag set. The buffer must have at
     * least {@code DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD + marker.endTxnMarkerValueSize()} bytes
     * of remaining capacity.
     *
     * @param buffer               destination buffer; must not be {@code null}
     * @param initialOffset        base offset of the control batch
     * @param timestamp            timestamp to assign to the control record, in milliseconds since epoch
     * @param partitionLeaderEpoch leader epoch of the partition, or {@link RecordBatch#NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH}
     * @param producerId           the producer id of the transaction being ended
     * @param producerEpoch        the producer epoch of the transaction being ended
     * @param marker               the end-of-transaction marker specifying commit or abort
     */
    public static void writeEndTransactionalMarker(ByteBuffer buffer, long initialOffset, long timestamp,
                                                   int partitionLeaderEpoch, long producerId, short producerEpoch,
                                                   EndTransactionMarker marker) {

After
    /**
     * Build a {@code MemoryRecords} containing a single KRaft {@link LeaderChangeMessage} control batch.
     *
     * <p>Leader change messages are written to the {@code __cluster_metadata} topic to record
     * leader elections in a KRaft cluster. The batch uses the current magic version with no
     * compression and the control flag set.
     *
     * @param initialOffset       base offset of the control batch
     * @param timestamp           timestamp of the record, in milliseconds since epoch
     * @param leaderEpoch         leader epoch at the time of writing
     * @param buffer              destination buffer for the serialized batch; must have sufficient capacity
     * @param leaderChangeMessage the leader change message to embed as a control record
     * @return a new {@code MemoryRecords} containing the leader change control batch
     */
    public static MemoryRecords withLeaderChangeMessage(
        long initialOffset,
        long timestamp,
        int leaderEpoch,
        ByteBuffer buffer,
        LeaderChangeMessage leaderChangeMessage
    ) {

    /**
     * Build a {@code MemoryRecords} containing a single KRaft {@link SnapshotHeaderRecord} control batch.
     *
     * <p>Snapshot header records mark the beginning of a KRaft snapshot written to the
     * {@code __cluster_metadata} topic. The batch uses the current magic version with no
     * compression and the control flag set.
     *
     * @param initialOffset        base offset of the control batch
     * @param timestamp            timestamp of the record, in milliseconds since epoch
     * @param leaderEpoch          leader epoch at the time of writing
     * @param buffer               destination buffer for the serialized batch; must have sufficient capacity
     * @param snapshotHeaderRecord the snapshot header record to embed as a control record
     * @return a new {@code MemoryRecords} containing the snapshot header control batch
     */

        long timestamp,
        int leaderEpoch,
        ByteBuffer buffer,

    /**
     * Build a {@code MemoryRecords} containing a single {@link KRaftVersionRecord} control batch.
     *
     * <p>KRaft version records are written to the {@code __cluster_metadata} topic to advertise
     * the KRaft protocol version in use by the cluster. The batch uses the current magic version
     * with no compression and the control flag set.
     *
     * @param initialOffset      base offset of the control batch
     * @param timestamp          timestamp of the record, in milliseconds since epoch
     * @param leaderEpoch        leader epoch at the time of writing
     * @param buffer             destination buffer for the serialized batch; must have sufficient capacity
     * @param kraftVersionRecord the KRaft version record to embed as a control record
     * @return a new {@code MemoryRecords} containing the KRaft version control batch
     */

        long initialOffset,
        long timestamp,
        int leaderEpoch,
        ByteBuffer buffer,
        KRaftVersionRecord kraftVersionRecord
    ) {

After
    /**
     * Build a {@code MemoryRecords} containing a single {@link VotersRecord} control batch.
     *
     * <p>Voters records are written to the {@code __cluster_metadata} topic to record the current
     * set of KRaft voters (i.e. the Raft quorum membership). The batch uses the current magic
     * version with no compression and the control flag set.
     *
     * @param initialOffset base offset of the control batch
     * @param timestamp     timestamp of the record, in milliseconds since epoch
     * @param leaderEpoch   leader epoch at the time of writing
     * @param buffer        destination buffer for the serialized batch; must have sufficient capacity
     * @param votersRecord  the voters record to embed as a control record
     * @return a new {@code MemoryRecords} containing the voters control batch
     */
    public static MemoryRecords withVotersRecord(
        long initialOffset,
        long timestamp,
        int leaderEpoch,
        ByteBuffer buffer,
        VotersRecord votersRecord
    ) {
39. WRITE /workspace/documentation.md
# Javadoc Generation Summary: Kafka Record Batch Serialization

## Scope

Three classes in `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/` were documented:

| File | Type | Magic version |
|---|---|---|
| `RecordBatch.java` | Interface | v0 / v1 / v2 |
| `DefaultRecordBatch.java` | Concrete class | v2+ |
| `MemoryRecords.java` | Concrete class | all |

---

## RecordBatch.java

### Class-level Javadoc (replaced)
The original two-sentence description was expanded to cover:
- **Role in the Kafka protocol** — the interface is the fundamental unit of data transfer used by producers, brokers, and consumers.
- **Magic version history** — v0 (no timestamps), v1 (per-record timestamps, single-record batches without compression), v2 (batch header, delta encoding, CRC-32C, idempotent/transactional support).
- **Usage guidance** — when to prefer `streamingIterator` vs `iterator`, and the requirement to close the streaming iterator.
- **Thread-safety** — explicitly stated as *not thread-safe*; `DefaultRecordBatch` wraps a shared `ByteBuffer`.
- **`@see` cross-references** to `DefaultRecordBatch`, `MemoryRecords`, and `Record`.

### Methods improved
| Method | Change |
|---|---|
| `hasProducerId()` | Added `@return` tag (was missing) |
| `isCompressed()` | Expanded `@return`; added note on lazy vs eager decompression trade-off |
| `writeTo(ByteBuffer)` | Clarified buffer capacity requirement and position advance behaviour |
| `isTransactional()` | Added explanation of `read_committed` consumer isolation and `EndTransactionMarker` |
| `isControlBatch()` | Added examples of control records (transaction markers, KRaft metadata); noted v0/v1 behaviour |
| `offsetOfMaxTimestamp()` | Rewrote the ad-hoc comment block into standard Javadoc with `@return`, noted per-call dec
... (truncated)
